title: Evaluation Task Sync and Portable Task Schema

implementationGate.rule: Implementation MUST NOT start until ADR-0002 is Accepted or a documented exception is approved.

contractReviewGate.rule: API-affecting implementation MUST NOT start until draft sync-ingest OpenAPI and portable-task JSON Schema artifacts have been reviewed for compatibility and security.

scope.primaryGoal: Persist evaluation-generated compliance tasks in a canonical, portable schema that remains compatible with FS-0001 clients and future editor surfaces.
nonGoals: Replace the current PDF viewer with BlockSuite
Require prompt redesign when current output can be mapped
Store full raw document bodies by default

must.text: Introduce explicit sync contract from existing backend evaluation/orchestrator output into durable task persistence.
Version and validate sync contract before persistence.
Use PostgreSQL persistence, extending pg-naviate-agent-gateway when governance and compatibility checks pass.
If pg-naviate-agent-gateway is unsuitable, preserve the same logical contract and record the alternative via ADR update before implementation proceeds.
Make sync idempotent for repeated submissions of the same evaluation run for the same document.
Persist stable task identity, document identity, evaluation-run identity, schema version, status, severity, citation/reference data, anchor data, provenance, and timestamps.
Retain user-managed task state separately enough to preserve compatible reviewer actions across reruns.
Mark superseded or obsolete generated tasks explicitly instead of silently hard-deleting active history.
Keep existing FS-0001 task retrieval, status update, citation-context, and rerun APIs supported behind compatible contracts.
Define an editor-neutral canonical task JSON schema with no required Word-, PDF-, or BlockSuite-specific core fields.
Support current PDF-viewer and future BlockSuite consumption through optional surface-specific extensions layered on canonical task data.
Include portable anchor/reference structures able to represent source excerpts, document locations, and optional editor-specific bindings.
Define contract artifacts for sync ingest, task snapshot read, task status overlay update, and rerun reconciliation behavior.
Reject unsupported schema versions or malformed payloads with actionable validation errors and no partial silent persistence.
Trace every persisted task back to its originating evaluation run and source document.
Audit sync attempts, deduplicated submissions, reconciliation outcomes, and task-status changes.
Support backward-compatible rollout from the current in-memory FS-0001 implementation to durable persistence.
If pg-naviate-agent-gateway is reused, FS-0002 persistence must not break existing Revit or Web Agent MCP server-registration workloads already using that database.

Shared-database reuse must isolate FS-0002 objects through a dedicated schema, dedicated roles, and migration boundaries that do not modify or drop existing MCP registration objects — including the currently evidenced UserMcpServers, UserSecrets, and DataProtectionKeys tables used by NaviateGateway — unless a separately approved migration plan covers them.

Before implementation begins against pg-naviate-agent-gateway, the team must inventory existing MCP registration tables, schemas, roles, and critical queries used by Revit/Web Agent flows and verify that FS-0002 changes are non-breaking against that catalog. The current minimum protected catalog includes UserMcpServers, UserSecrets, DataProtectionKeys, and the ShareWithRevit sharing behavior evidenced in the existing gateway code.

should.text: Expose a stable logical task key for cross-run matching even when task IDs are regenerated.
Provide a task projection optimized for list UIs while preserving access to the full canonical record.
Support explicit task lifecycle markers such as superseded, reopened, or carried_forward for diagnostics.
Support batch sync and retrieval for multi-document evaluation scenarios without changing per-document canonical schema.

may.text: Publish task-sync events for downstream analytics or notifications.
Support export adapters that transform canonical task data into editor-specific packages.

authModel.syncProducerIdentity: backend managed identity or approved service principal/workload identity
authModel.userFacingIdentity: Microsoft 365 delegated user identity via SSO/OAuth
authModel.compatibilityAuthorizationFloor: preserve the current FS-0001 authorization baseline until a narrower approved scope model replaces it
authModel.rendererAccessRule: clients must access task data through approved backend APIs and must not receive direct database credentials

governancePolicies: Test and Development Data Handling Procedure
must: Use authenticated backend trust boundaries for sync ingestion.
Enforce tenant, user, and document scoping for task reads and writes.
Persist only minimum task evidence and not full raw document bodies by default.
Apply PII redaction before persistence where configured.
Use HTTPS/TLS for sync, read, and update traffic.
Retain task, audit, and sync metadata for at least 30 days.
Update threat model for durable persistence, replay/idempotency, rerun reconciliation, and multi-client consumption.
Enable dependency, migration, and vulnerability checks in CI.
Enforce payload size limits and schema validation before database writes.
Use least-privilege database access and approved secret management patterns.
should: Connect evaluation run, sync attempt, persistence write, and user-visible task actions with correlation IDs.
Use immutable or append-only audit semantics for sync receipts and task-state transitions where practical.
Rely on approved platform encryption and backup settings for the chosen PostgreSQL target.

fieldRules: description, citation, and PortableAnchor.excerpt contain remediation-relevant snippets only and must respect approved maximum lengths
TaskStateOverlay.resolutionNote and TaskActionAudit delta fields must be truncated or redacted before persistence when they may contain free text
userId values in audit or export contexts must use approved internal identifiers and must not expose unnecessary profile attributes
reviewer notes and renderer-specific extensions must be excluded from broad client projections unless the caller is authorized to view them

retention.syncReceiptsAndTaskActionAudits: minimum 30 days, default purge/archive ceiling 180 days unless legal hold or approved exception applies

controlMatrix.policy: Test and Development Data Handling Procedure
Test and Development Data Handling Procedure

required: Sync request counts, success/failure, and deduplicated replay counts
Validation-failure reasons without prohibited raw content
Task snapshot and status update latency/failure rates

integrationConstraints: Existing backend evaluation/orchestrator output is the source for generated tasks.
Existing FS-0001 endpoints remain externally compatible and may be reimplemented over durable persistence.
The portable task schema must be reusable by both the current PDF viewer and a future BlockSuite editor.
Persistence should extend pg-naviate-agent-gateway when feasible, but the logical task contract must not be coupled to a single infrastructure assumption.

Existing Revit and Web Agent MCP server-registration data in the current gateway implementation is backed by PostgreSQL tables UserMcpServers and UserSecrets, plus DataProtectionKeys for protected secret handling; these must remain operational and isolated from FS-0002 schema evolution.

unit: Logical task-key matching and rerun reconciliation rules
integration: Evaluation output to sync ingest to PostgreSQL persistence
Existing FS-0001-compatible task-read and task-update APIs over persistent storage
Existing FS-0001-compatible citation-context API over persistent storage
Existing FS-0001-compatible rerun API over persistent storage
Shared-database regression coverage proving existing Revit/Web Agent MCP registration paths remain unaffected when FS-0002 objects exist in the same PostgreSQL server
Replay submission handling and dedup receipt creation
Migration/fallback behavior from in-memory service to persistent storage
endToEnd: Evaluate a document, persist tasks, load tasks in an existing client, update status, rerun evaluation, and verify carried-forward state
Retrieve citation context and rerun tasks from an FS-0001-compatible client against the persistence-backed implementation
Render the same canonical task payload in the current PDF viewer and a compatibility harness representing future BlockSuite consumption
security: Authorization and trust-boundary enforcement for sync ingestion
Data minimization and PII redaction verification
Audit retention and privileged database-access checks

contractsToProduce: OpenAPI for sync ingest, canonical task snapshot read, task status/state overlay update, and rerun reconciliation
JSON schema for the portable canonical task payload with extension points for PDF viewer and future BlockSuite editor
Persistence model documentation covering task identity, evaluation-run linkage, and reconciliation rules
Draft security review notes covering authorization model, field-level privacy handling, and PostgreSQL target suitability before API-affecting implementation begins
Shared-database compatibility notes documenting existing MCP registration dependencies, the non-breaking isolation strategy, and rollback checks if pg-naviate-agent-gateway is reused

acceptanceCriteria.given: an evaluation run completes through the existing backend/orchestrator flow
acceptanceCriteria.when: the sync contract is invoked with a valid portable task payload
acceptanceCriteria.then: the evaluation run, sync receipt, and canonical task records are persisted and become available through compatible task-read APIs

acceptanceCriteria.given: a previously processed evaluation run for the same document
acceptanceCriteria.when: the identical sync payload is submitted again
acceptanceCriteria.then: the system records a deduplicated sync receipt and does not create duplicate active task records

acceptanceCriteria.given: a reviewer has updated task statuses for a document
acceptanceCriteria.when: a later evaluation rerun produces matching or equivalent tasks
acceptanceCriteria.then: compatible user-managed state is carried forward according to documented reconciliation rules

acceptanceCriteria.given: an FS-0001-compatible client requests tasks or updates task status
acceptanceCriteria.then: the client continues to function without a breaking contract change

acceptanceCriteria.given: the canonical task payload for a document
acceptanceCriteria.when: the current PDF viewer and a future BlockSuite editor or approved compatibility harness consume the payload
acceptanceCriteria.then: both resolve the same task meaning from canonical fields while using optional surface-specific extensions only where needed

acceptanceCriteria.given: a sync payload is malformed or uses an unsupported schema version
acceptanceCriteria.when: the sync endpoint validates the payload
acceptanceCriteria.then: the request fails with actionable errors and no partial task persistence occurs

acceptanceCriteria.given: sync, reconciliation, and user task actions occur
acceptanceCriteria.when: operators inspect logs and audit records
acceptanceCriteria.then: correlation IDs, sync receipts, and task-action audit data allow end-to-end tracing

acceptanceCriteria.given: an FS-0001-compatible client requests citation/reference context for a persisted task
acceptanceCriteria.then: the client receives a compatible citation-context response without a breaking contract change

acceptanceCriteria.given: an FS-0001-compatible client invokes manual rerun for a document with persisted tasks
acceptanceCriteria.then: the rerun completes through compatible request/response semantics and returns refreshed task state without a breaking contract change

acceptanceCriteria.given: pg-naviate-agent-gateway is already used by Revit and Web Agent MCP server-registration flows backed by UserMcpServers, UserSecrets, and DataProtectionKeys
acceptanceCriteria.when: FS-0002 persistence is introduced into that same database
acceptanceCriteria.then: existing registration reads and writes continue to succeed unchanged, and FS-0002 objects remain isolated to approved schema and role boundaries

environmentRequirements: production task persistence must be segregated from lower environments
approved PostgreSQL target must document networking, backup/restore, access-role boundaries, and environment ownership before go-live
reuse of pg-naviate-agent-gateway requires an explicit suitability checklist covering tenancy, lifecycle ownership, capacity, backup posture, and governance approval

the suitability checklist must include compatibility with existing Revit/Web Agent MCP registration workloads, including schema isolation, role isolation, migration rollback, and regression verification of the current UserMcpServers/UserSecrets/DataProtectionKeys paths and ShareWithRevit behavior
compatibility: future BlockSuite editor via extension mappings

openQuestions: Can pg-naviate-agent-gateway host the new schema and workload without governance or lifecycle conflicts?
What stable logical task-key strategy should be used for cross-run matching?
Should reconciliation markers such as superseded remain internal-only or become externally visible diagnostics?
What extension namespace convention should be used for editor-specific enrichments?

Does pg-naviate-agent-gateway currently host any additional Revit/Web Agent MCP registration tables beyond UserMcpServers, UserSecrets, and DataProtectionKeys, and what exact non-breaking coexistence plan is required if reuse proceeds?
assumptions: Existing backend evaluation/orchestrator output can be mapped into the portable task schema without a prompt redesign.
FS-0001 remains the current consumer contract baseline and must not be broken by FS-0002.
BlockSuite compatibility is a schema portability target, not a system-of-record requirement.
